title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure AWS Management Console authentication failures are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for failed console authentication attempts.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring failed console logins may decrease the lead time to detect an attempt to brute-force a credential, which may provide an indicator, such as the source IP address, that can be used in other event correlations.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: Internal Only - General o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }" 4. Note the <console-signin-failure-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <console-signin-failure-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<console-signin-failure-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: Internal Only - General 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS management Console login failures and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<console-signin-failure-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <console-signin-failure-alarm> --metric-name <console-signin-failure-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Monitoring for these failures may generate a large number of alerts, especially in larger environments.
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79191-3 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html Internal Only - General 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_logs_console_authentication_failures_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **List all CloudTrail trails:** Use the AWS CLI command:
   ```bash
   aws cloudtrail describe-trails
   ```
   to retrieve a list of trails and their properties.

2. **Identify multi-region trails:** Filter the trails where `"IsMultiRegionTrail"` is set to `true`.

3. **Get the log group name for active multi-region trails:** Use the property:
   ```json
   "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn"
   ```
   to extract the log group name (e.g., `arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log-group:<trail-log-group-name>`).

4. **Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active:** Use:
   ```bash
   aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>
   ```
   Check that `"IsLogging"` is `TRUE`.

5. **Ensure the trail captures management events:** Use:
   ```bash
   aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>
   ```
   Verify that there is at least one event selector with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

6. **List associated metric filters for the log group:** Use:
   ```bash
   aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>
   ```
   Check for a filter with:
   ```json
   "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = \"Failed authentication\") }"
   ```

7. **Verify the metric associated with failed console sign-in attempts:** Get the `metricName` from the filter matched above.

8. **List CloudWatch alarms and filter:** Use:
   ```bash
   aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<console-signin-failure-metric>]'
   ```
   Check that at least one alarm exists referencing this metric.

9. **Check SNS topic associated with alarms:** Capture the `AlarmActions` from the previous step and validate the SNS Topic ARN.

10. **Ensure at least one active subscriber for the SNS topic:** Use:
    ```bash
    aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>
    ```
    Verify that at least one subscription has a valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- The control passes if:
  - A multi-region CloudTrail trail is active and logs authentication attempts.
  - There exists a metric filter capturing failed console logins.
  - There is a CloudWatch alarm associated with this metric.
  - The SNS topic has at least one active subscription.
  
Each step's expected output and criteria for success need to be explicitly checked to ensure compliance with the control.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE
- The classification as "Automated" is mostly correct, as the steps outlined can be executed using AWS CLI commands and could be automated using scripts or AWS SDKs like boto3. However, the initial setup of CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS configurations might require some manual intervention, especially if these services are not already configured.
- All checks described can be performed using AWS APIs/boto3/CLI, which supports the automated classification.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate, and the AWS CLI commands used are valid for the described tasks.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_logs_console_authentication_failures_monitoring` is descriptive but could be more concise.
- The steps are comprehensive, covering the necessary checks for the control, but there are minor improvements that could be made for clarity and completeness.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name:** Consider a more concise name such as `aws_cloudtrail_console_auth_failures_monitoring`.
- **Step 3:** Clarify that the `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn` needs to be parsed to extract the log group name.
- **Step 6:** The filter pattern should be verified for accuracy. It might be beneficial to include a broader pattern to capture variations in error messages.
- **Step 7 & 8:** Ensure that the metric name is correctly captured and used in subsequent steps. Consider using a more descriptive metric name for clarity.
- **Additional Considerations:** Include a check to ensure that the CloudWatch Logs are being delivered to the specified log group. This could be done by verifying the log delivery status in CloudTrail.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS security best practices. The use of AWS CLI commands is appropriate for the tasks described, and the steps cover the necessary checks for compliance with the control.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Improve the program name for clarity and conciseness. Ensure the filter pattern is robust enough to capture all relevant failed authentication attempts. Add a check for log delivery status to ensure completeness.
- The classification as "Automated" is appropriate, but note that initial setup might require manual steps if not already configured.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** The task of monitoring AWS Management Console authentication failures can be fully automated using AWS services such as CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS. Both AI opinions agree that the necessary checks and configurations can be executed using AWS CLI commands or AWS SDKs like boto3. The initial setup of these services might require some manual configuration if not already in place, but the monitoring itself can be automated. The program name suggested by the second AI opinion is more concise and appropriate.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_console_auth_failures_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, SNS
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status`
  - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors`
  - `aws logs describe-metric-filters`
  - `aws logs put-metric-filter`
  - `aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - Multi-region trail status (`IsMultiRegionTrail`)
  - Logging status (`IsLogging`)
  - Event selectors (`IncludeManagementEvents`, `ReadWriteType`)
  - Metric filter pattern (`{ ($.eventName = ConsoleLogin) && ($.errorMessage = "Failed authentication") }`)
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with logging enabled
  - Metric filter for failed console logins is present and correctly configured
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - No active multi-region CloudTrail trail
  - Missing or incorrectly configured metric filter for failed console logins

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** The second AI opinion suggested a more concise program name and highlighted the need for clarity in some steps. These suggestions were incorporated to enhance the automation process.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v4.0.1
